summary: Frontend regression test of the 3 Messages/DM bug fixes: (1) case-insensitive owner/staff DM lookup, (2) click-toggle voice message recording, (3) WebRTC audio playback (optional). Bug 3 passes fully. Bug 2 verified at the code level (correct mime negotiation, correct NotAllowedError/NotFoundError messages, click-toggle behaviour intact) but the end-to-end recording->upload->playback path is INCONCLUSIVE because headless Chromium in the sandbox has no microphone device (NotFoundError). Bug 1 (call modal) is also INCONCLUSIVE for the same reason. Regression flows (threads list, block/unblock toggle, message send) all work.

issues: React warning spam: 'Encountered two children with the same key' repeats continuously in console once Messages view opens. Likely caused by non-unique key in threads.map / messages.map / searchResults.map (or a parent list). Not blocking, but pollutes logs and can hide real errors — please fix by ensuring unique keys (e.g., dedupe threads by other_id, dedupe messages by id in the 2s poller which appends new deltas that may overlap with the initial load).

action_items: Investigate and fix the repeating React 'duplicate key' warning in MessagesView — the 2s poller in useEffect may be appending messages already in the initial load, producing duplicate ids. Suggest de-duping by id when merging: `combined = Array.from(new Map([...m, ...new].map(x => [x.id, x])).values())`.
critical_code_review_comments: MessagesView poller merges initial load + since-based deltas without de-duping by message id — leads to the repeated React key warning we observed and, over time, duplicate rendered bubbles.

openUserByName() lower-cases the username in the URL path (`/messages/user/${uname}`) before calling — this is fine because backend does case-insensitive regex, but if you ever need it exact for another endpoint the frontend is silently mangling input.

startCall/acceptCall's try/catch shows a toast then calls endCall(), but at that point `call` state may already be set → endCall dispatches an 'end' signal to the peer even though no offer was ever sent. Consider only sending 'end' when pcRef.current existed.

Voice recording's stream cleanup lives inside rec.onstop — if the recorder never actually starts (e.g., NotFoundError early return), the stream is fine (never obtained). But rec.onerror path does not stop the tracks — potential leak. Add stream.getTracks().forEach(t=>t.stop()) in the onerror branch.

success_rate.frontend: Bug 3: 100% pass. Bug 2: code paths verified, e2e inconclusive (no mic hw). Bug 1: inconclusive (no mic hw). Regressions: 100%.

test_credentials: testbugfix1 / password1 — worked. Owner Balkin resolvable via case-insensitive search from a regular user.
seed_data_creation: None. A few DMs to Balkin were sent as part of the flow ('hello owner') and one voice-msg audio element appeared from a prior test iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug 2 and Bug 1 require a real microphone to fully verify end-to-end. Headless Chromium reports NotFoundError so the error path is exercised but not the happy path. If a virtual mic (fake-device-for-media-stream + fake-ui-for-media-stream flags) is needed, launch Chromium with --use-fake-ui-for-media-stream --use-fake-device-for-media-stream. In this iteration BrowserContext.grant_permissions was granted but no virtual audio device was injected.

bug3_case_insensitive_search.send_text_hello_owner: PASS — message bubble right-aligned (justify-end), text present in DOM

bug2_voice_recording.start_click_result: toast 'No microphone found.' (NotFoundError path) — expected in headless without a mic device
bug2_voice_recording.voice_bubble_after_stop: 1 existing (prior test artifact) — new upload not confirmed
bug2_voice_recording.click_toggle_wiring: data-testid=voice-btn uses onClick={toggleRecording}, verified in code (line 550 of MessagesView.jsx)
bug2_voice_recording.verdict: INCONCLUSIVE — code and click-toggle wiring correct; error messaging correct; but happy path can't be exercised without real mic.

bug1_call_modal.call_audio_btn_click: startCall → getUserMedia rejects NotFoundError → toast 'Can\'t access mic/cam: Requested device not found' → endCall() closes modal immediately
bug1_call_modal.verdict: INCONCLUSIVE — cannot verify Calling… modal without a mic device.

regressions.block_btn: PASS — clicking block turns btn red (bg-red-500/20 text-red-400), unblock toast fires; note the assertion for 'red bg gone' returned false but that's because default class contains 'hover:bg-red-500/20' — functional block/unblock works (toast 'Unblocked @Balkin' visible).
